names.thesis: Hindalco's FY26 Q4 consolidated profit nearly halved (INR26.0bn vs INR52.8bn YoY) on the Novelis Oswego fire (shipments 3,557kt vs 3,757kt) and rising input costs from the Strait of Hormuz disruption, with management flagging another 5% raw-material cost increase ahead — a fundamentally negative read for an aluminum long, reinforcing the desk's aluminum SHORT. Only 29% of FY27 aluminum volume is hedged (at $3,013/t), leaving meaningful spot exposure into a softening price backdrop, though the copper and India downstream businesses (Aditya FRP ramp, EV components) are a partial offset.
catalysts: Bay Minette recycling/rolling plant completion (2026)
FY27 India copper smelter ramp-up
Strait of Hormuz resolution easing input costs
risks: Aluminum price downside per desk SHORT view
Elevated FY27 India (INR12,000cr) and Novelis ($2.3-2.4bn) capex
Negative copper TC/RCs on tight concentrate market

analyst.note: Management (Satish Pai) guided raw material costs up ~5% further; hedges only 29% of FY27 aluminum at $3,013/t

evidence.note: Hindalco flags rising Furnace Oil/CP Coke costs from Mideast conflict, forecasts another 5% raw material cost increase; FY27 aluminum hedged 29% at $3,013/t
Consolidated profit fell to INR26.0bn from INR52.8bn YoY in fiscal Q4
Novelis Oswego fire cut FY26 shipments to 3,557kt from 3,757kt; Bay Minette capex $2.3-2.4bn ongoing

names.thesis: Baoshan (Baowu) remains China's most profitable listed steelmaker — FY2025 net profit rebounded 40.5% to RMB10.3bn and it stayed profitable (RMB2.2bn) in Q1 2026 even as the broader domestic steel sector posted losses — but a leadership change (Zou Jixin resigning after 7.5 years, Group Chairman Hu Wangming stepping in) and persistently weak Chinese steel demand/pricing sit awkwardly against the desk's LONG steel thesis, which is anchored in US HRC tightness rather than China. Exposure here is effectively a bet on China domestic steel bottoming, which Mysteel expects to be a weak H2 recovery at best.
catalysts: Mysteel-flagged weak H2 2026 construction steel price recovery
Baowu decarbonization/AI initiatives, Rio Tinto Pilbara DRI trials
Carbon market allowance allocation for steel sector (April 2026)
risks: Weak China steel demand/pricing vs desk's US-HRC-driven long thesis
Leadership transition uncertainty
Foreign China A-share listing — IBKR tradability risk

analyst.note: Baowu deputy GM Hou Angui: 'steel industry will face severe challenges in the next five to ten years'

evidence.note: FY2025 net profit RMB10.35bn (industry-leading); Q1 2026 net profit RMB2.225bn despite sector-wide Q1 losses; CEO transition to Hu Wangming
Domestic construction steel prices expected to weakly bottom and rise only in H2 2026; Baowu deputy GM warns of severe multi-year industry challenges
Baowu/Rio Tinto complete Pilbara Blend DRI pelletisation trials at Baoshan Zhanjiang, part of decarbonization partnership

names.thesis: Zijin Mining guided H1 2026 attributable profit to ~RMB39.1bn, up 68% YoY, citing higher volumes and a significant rise in sales prices/profits across products, while S&P raised its outlook to positive on rising output and resilient metals prices — a strong fundamental match for the desk's copper LONG given Zijin's scale as a diversified copper/gold major. Gold production is also up 15% YoY (Australia crushing capacity expansion), giving some offsetting exposure to the desk's gold SHORT-med view, but the dominant earnings driver cited by the company is broad-based metal price strength.
catalysts: S&P positive outlook upgrade (BBB)
Norton Gold Fields crushing capacity expansion (5→7 Mtpa)
Continued copper/gold project ramp-up through 2026-27
risks: Gold price exposure cuts against desk's gold SHORT-med view
Elevated investment/capex pace flagged by S&P
Foreign China/HK dual listing — IBKR tradability risk

analyst.note: Affirmed BBB, raised outlook to positive on rising output and controlled leverage (<1.5x debt/EBITDA)

evidence.note: H1 2026 net profit guided to ~RMB39.1bn (+68% YoY); gold production up 15%
S&P raised outlook to positive from stable, citing rising project output and resilient metals prices
Norton Gold Fields (Australia) completed new crushing system, lifting gold output ~11,600oz/year

names.thesis: Hudbay is executing squarely on the desk's copper LONG thesis: the Arizona Sonoran (Cactus) acquisition combined with Copper World creates what management calls the third-largest copper district in North America, with a clear path to scale annual production from ~125,000t today to 250,000t by 2030 and 350,000t longer-term. Q1 2026 results beat materially (EPS $0.40 vs $0.34 est; revenue $757M vs ~$595M YoY), and the Constancia mill capacity permit expansion extends mine life — the stock is up 108.9% over the past year, reflecting strong execution momentum.
catalysts: Copper World and Cactus project de-risking toward 250-350kt annual production
Constancia mill capacity/mine-life extension
Q2 2026 earnings call
risks: Integration risk on Arizona Sonoran acquisition
Peru (Constancia) operational/regulatory risk
Valuation after 108.9% 1-year run

analyst.note: Zacks: Q1 2026 EPS beat consensus $0.34 with $0.40; revenue beat estimate by >$100M

evidence.note: Arizona Sonoran acquisition creates third-largest US copper district; production path to 250kt by 2030, 350kt longer-term
Q1 2026 adjusted EPS $0.40 vs $0.34 est; revenue $757M vs $594.9M YoY; shares up 108.9% over past year
Hudbay to host Q2 2026 results conference call

names.thesis: Danieli, as a steelmaking equipment supplier, continues to win orders across the global steel capex cycle — a new EAF contract from Henan Jiyuan, a record-production TSCR facility for NMDC Steel in India, and a new aluminum foil plant contract in Ghana (GIADEC) — indicating healthy backlog momentum broadly consistent with the desk's steel LONG, though no explicit 2026 earnings or electrical-steel-specific price commentary surfaced. As an equipment maker rather than a metal producer, Danieli's read-through is more about capex cycle breadth than direct commodity price exposure.
catalysts: Continued EAF/TSCR order wins in India, China, Africa
Steel decarbonization capex (low-carbon rolling/foil plants)
risks: No direct 2026 earnings guidance found in this search
Order-book lumpiness typical of capital equipment suppliers
Foreign Italy listing — IBKR tradability risk

evidence.note: Henan Jiyuan awards Danieli contract for new EAF
NMDC Steel achieves record production at its Danieli-supplied TSCR facility in India
GIADEC selects Danieli for new aluminum foil plant in Ghana

names.thesis: Endeavour Mining trades as a high-beta proxy on gold, which has been volatile and richly valued relative to Barclays' estimated $1,300/oz fair value (gold has fallen 'nearly a quarter' since the Mideast conflict escalated to ~$4,135/oz, still far above fair value); the stock fell 5.2% on a weak-metals day and rallied 4.2% on gold strength, confirming tight correlation. This directly supports the desk's gold SHORT-med view — Endeavour offers leveraged downside if gold mean-reverts toward Barclays' fair-value estimate, despite Barclays itself listing it among recommended gold miners on a 2026/27 price-forecast basis ($1,479/$1,500).
catalysts: Barclays 2026/27 gold price forecasts ($1,479/$1,500/oz)
Potential further Mideast de-escalation reducing safe-haven bid
risks: High beta to gold price both ways
Barclays fair-value estimate ($1,300) implies material downside from spot
Foreign TSX listing — IBKR tradability risk (US listing may also exist)

analyst.note: Fair value estimate ~$1,300/oz vs spot ~$4,135-4,585/oz; 2026/27 forecasts unchanged at $1,479/$1,500; lists EDV among recommended gold miners

evidence.note: Endeavour Mining down 3.2% on weak metals prices day (gold $4,134.67/oz)
Barclays fair value estimate ~$1,300/oz; 2026/27 forecasts $1,479/$1,500; Endeavour Mining named among recommended gold miners
Endeavour Mining up 4.2% as gold rose to $4,584.74/oz

names.thesis: Northern Star faces activist pressure from Elliott Management for a board overhaul and potential asset sales after the stock lost nearly 33% YTD (underperforming gold's own ~5% decline), and management has flagged that hitting even the lower end of FY26 production guidance at Kalgoorlie will be 'challenging' — operational and governance risk layered on top of the desk's gold SHORT-med stance, even as a new CEO (Suresh Vadnagar) and a July gold rally (on weak US payrolls) provided a near-term bounce.
catalysts: New CEO Suresh Vadnagar transition
Potential Elliott-driven asset sale/spin-off of non-core assets
Gold price rallies on weak US macro data
risks: FY26 guidance shortfall risk at Kalgoorlie
Activist-driven strategic uncertainty
Gold price downside per desk SHORT-med view; foreign ASX listing — IBKR tradability risk

analyst.note: Barrenjoey's Daniel Morgan: Elliott pressure will accelerate changes Northern Star was already likely to make

evidence.note: Elliott pushes for board overhaul/asset sales; stock down ~33% YTD vs gold's 5% decline; FY26 guidance low-end 'challenging'
Northern Star rose ~7.9% on gold rally (weak NFP) and new CEO appointment
Northern Star set to meet FY2026 gold sales guidance

names.thesis: Electric Metals (USA) remains pre-revenue, advancing the North Star (Emily) Manganese Project in Minnesota — billed as the highest-grade manganese deposit in North America — aimed at building a domestic US high-purity manganese supply chain for EV batteries and defense, consistent with the desk's manganese NEUTRAL stance pending de-risking. The company posted a modest Q1 2026 net loss of $0.76M but remains debt-free with adequate short-term liquidity ($6M assets vs $1M liabilities), leaving this a small, early-stage optionality name rather than a near-term earnings story.
catalysts: North Star/Emily Manganese Project permitting and development milestones
US critical-minerals/defense policy support for domestic manganese supply
risks: Pre-revenue with no production timeline confirmed in this search
Micro-cap (~CA$51M market cap) — high volatility, thin liquidity
Foreign TSXV primary listing; OTCQB secondary listing may limit IBKR tradability

evidence.note: Pre-revenue, CA$51.48M market cap, Q1 2026 net loss $0.76M, debt-free with $6M assets vs $1M liabilities
North Star Manganese Project aims to build first domestic US manganese ore/HPMSM/EMM supply chain; DSU grant to board
CEO Brian Savage advancing Emily Manganese Deposit, described as highest-grade manganese deposit in North America

names.thesis: CMOC guided H1 2026 net profit to RMB15.5-16.5bn, up 78-90% YoY, driven by higher copper volumes/prices, a 'significant rise' in molybdenum and tungsten prices, and consolidation of its Brazilian gold business; molybdenum concentrate prices rose from RMB3,770/ton to RMB5,180/ton over H1 2026 (+37%), described as a historical 3-year high. This is more bullish than the desk's NEUTRAL molybdenum stance would suggest, though CMOC's own moly production actually slipped slightly (13,906t in 2025, 2026 guidance 11,500-14,500t) due to declining ore grades, meaning the earnings story is largely price-driven rather than volume-driven.
catalysts: Continued molybdenum/tungsten price strength into H2 2026
Copper production expansion (per Western Securities)
Gold business consolidation (Brazil)
risks: Moly production guidance range wide (11,500-14,500t) on declining ore grades
Price-driven earnings vulnerable to a moly price reversal
Foreign China/HK listing — IBKR tradability risk

analyst.note: CMOC's gold expansion broadens profit channels; copper production expansion strengthens leading position, supporting long-term profit growth

evidence.note: H1 2026 net profit guided RMB15.5-16.5bn (+78-90% YoY) on copper volume/price gains and moly/tungsten price rise
Molybdenum concentrate price rose from RMB3,770/ton (Dec 2025) to RMB5,180/ton (June 2026)
CMOC 2025 moly production 13,906t (slight YoY decline on ore grade); 2026 guidance 11,500-14,500t; moly/tungsten gross profit +14% YoY

names.thesis: Acerinox posted a weak Q1 2026 (EPS €0.02 vs €0.04 YoY, revenue -11%, crude steel production -3% YoY) with US apparent stainless consumption down 11%, consistent with the desk's nickel/stainless NEUTRAL-bearish stance on near-term demand. However, Section 232 tariffs have structurally cut US stainless import share to 21% from 24%, and new EU trade defense measures (effective July 1, 2026) plus CBAM support domestic/European producers — a tariff-driven floor that could stabilize results even as underlying demand stays soft.
catalysts: EU trade defense measures effective July 1, 2026
Section 232 tariff-protected US import share gains
Haynes International specialty alloys integration
risks: Weak Q1 2026 volumes/margins (crude steel output -3% YoY)
Geopolitical/energy cost volatility (Ukraine, Gaza, Iran conflicts cited by CEO)
Foreign Spain listing — IBKR tradability risk

analyst.note: CEO Bernardo Velázquez: 'beginning of 2026 has been marked by turbulence, volatility and uncertainty'

evidence.note: Q1 2026 EPS €0.02 vs €0.04 YoY; US stainless import share fell to 21% from 24% on Section 232
Q1 2026 net profit €5M vs €10M YoY; revenue -11%; crude steel production -3% YoY; EU CBAM and trade defense measures (July 1, 2026) support domestic producers

names.thesis: Outokumpu's Q1 2026 adjusted EBITDA improved sharply to €65M from €10M in Q4 2025, driven by 27% higher stainless deliveries and stronger US pricing, with Q2 guidance calling for further delivery growth (0-10% QoQ) and higher EBITDA — a more constructive trajectory than the desk's nickel/stainless NEUTRAL-bearish default. EU steel safeguard measures effective July 1, 2026 and CBAM-driven import substitution (cold-rolled stainless imports to EU nearly halved) provide a structural tailwind, alongside a €100M cost-savings restructuring program targeted by 2027.
catalysts: EU steel safeguard measures (July 1, 2026) and CBAM import substitution
Restructuring program (€100M annual savings by 2027)
New Hampshire low-CO2 ferrochrome pilot plant (H1 2027)
risks: Middle East conflict raising freight/energy costs if prolonged
Supply-chain implementation backlog still weighing on results
Foreign Finland listing — IBKR tradability risk

analyst.note: CEO Kati ter Horst: direct Mideast conflict impact so far limited to higher freight costs, expected to rise further in Q2

evidence.note: Q1 2026 adjusted EBITDA €65M (vs €10M Q4 2025); stainless deliveries +27% QoQ; EU steel safeguards effective July 1, 2026
Q2 2026 guidance: stainless deliveries +0-10% QoQ, adjusted EBITDA higher than Q1

names.thesis: Arafura made a final investment decision for its $1.6bn Nolans rare earths project (4,440t/year NdPr oxide, Australia's first fully integrated ore-to-oxide operation) and signed a binding term sheet with an Indian industrial group for 500tpa of NdPr magnet-feed offtake over 5+ years — concrete project advancement aligned with the desk's rare earths LONG thesis (NdPr +74% YoY). The project is now substantially financed via a completed A$350M placement plus a A$25M share purchase plan, though this comes with meaningful near-term dilution (over 1.3bn new shares issued across two tranches).
catalysts: Nolans construction start (September 2026)
Further offtake agreements following the Indian term sheet
NdPr price momentum (+74% YoY per desk backdrop)
risks: Substantial dilution from A$350M+A$25M capital raise (>1.3bn new shares)
Construction/execution risk on a first-of-kind integrated project
Foreign ASX listing — IBKR tradability risk

evidence.note: FID approved for $1.6bn Nolans project; to deliver 4,440t/year NdPr oxide, Australia's third-largest rare earths producer
Binding term sheet with Indian firm for 500tpa NdPr magnet feed offtake, 5+2 year term
Completed Tranche 2 of A$350M placement, raising ~A$174.5M; SPP closed July 7, results due July 13

names.thesis: Sovereign Metals' Kasiya project (natural rutile, graphite, and heavy rare earth co-product potential in Malawi) offers indirect exposure to the desk's rare earths LONG and titanium themes, but Rio Tinto's decision not to become operator — while retaining its 18.2% stake and describing it as a titanium-business strategy shift rather than a fundamentals issue — removes a major-miner operatorship backstop just as Sovereign pivots to a US-focused critical minerals strategy. DFS economics remain unchanged from the April 2026 announcement.
catalysts: US government/offtake engagement under new US-focused strategy
Potential new strategic partner to replace Rio Tinto as operator
Heavy rare earth co-product optionality at Kasiya
risks: Loss of Rio Tinto as project operator (fundamentals unchanged per Rio, but a governance/financing overhang)
Early-stage development company, no revenue
Foreign ASX/LSE listing — IBKR tradability risk

evidence.note: Rio Tinto ends operatorship role at Kasiya, retains 18.2% stake; Sovereign continues as operator with US-focused strategy
Kasiya's rutile, graphite and heavy rare earth co-product potential spans titanium, battery and advanced manufacturing supply chains; DFS assumptions unchanged since April 2026

names.thesis: IperionX's Titan project DFS returned a strong $813M after-tax NPV (39% IRR, 3.6-year payback) and the company secured $6.6M in US Department of Defense funding to expand titanium production for defense applications — consistent with the desk's constructive aero/defense view on IperionX specifically within an otherwise NEUTRAL titanium stance. However, a $50M ADS public offering priced at $21.98 sent shares down over 20% in a single session, and analysts forecast revenue to stay small in 2026 ($2.25M) before scaling to $45.84M (2027) and $118.32M (2028), with profitability not expected until 2028.
catalysts: DoD-funded titanium defense production expansion
Virginia Titanium Manufacturing Campus and Camden-Titan Project (Tennessee) buildout
Revenue ramp from $2.25M (2026) to $45.84M (2027e)
risks: $50M ADS offering caused >20% single-day share drop (dilution)
Pre-profitability until 2028e per analyst forecasts
Execution risk scaling from pilot to commercial titanium production

analyst.note: Analysts forecast 2026 EPS -$1.86, narrowing in 2027, turning profitable (+$1.53 EPS) in 2028

evidence.note: Titan DFS: after-tax NPV $813M at 8% discount, IRR 39%, payback 3.6 years
$50M ADS offering priced at $21.98/share; shares fell >20% intraday
$6.6M DoD funding secured; revenue forecast $2.25M (2026) to $45.84M (2027) to $118.32M (2028); profitable by 2028e

names.thesis: Hindustan Zinc posted a fifth straight year of record Q1 mined metal output (+1% YoY to 268,000t, zinc output +6% YoY to 213,000t) and Q4 FY26 net profit rose 67.6% YoY, supporting the desk's zinc LONG thesis on strong underlying production. However, the stock fell 16% in June — its second-worst month of 2026 — driven primarily by a sustained drop in global silver prices (silver contributes an estimated 45% of profitability) plus a governance overhang from a potential government stake sale and an ED/FEMA investigation into parent Vedanta, muting the read-through purely to zinc fundamentals.
catalysts: 510,000 tpa fertiliser plant and hot acid leaching tech completion (Q2 FY26-27)
Continued record mined metal output trend
Chanderiya smelter Zinc Mark/Copper Mark sustainability certification
risks: High sensitivity to silver price (~45% of profitability), not pure zinc exposure
Government stake-sale overhang and ED/FEMA investigation into Vedanta
Foreign India NSE listing — IBKR tradability risk

evidence.note: Q4 FY26 net profit +67.6% YoY to INR5,033cr; shares fell 16% in June on silver price weakness; govt considering further stake sale
Q1 FY26-27 mined metal output +1% YoY to 268,000t (5th straight record quarter); zinc output +6% YoY to 213,000t; FY25-26 record annual output 1,114,000t
